section.title: Tool index
section.intro: AI tools for research, writing, meetings, and field work. Organized from everyday to technical – start where you are.

resources.title: ChatGPT
resources.description: General-purpose LLM. Web browsing, code execution, file analysis, image generation. 300M+ users. Free tier uses inputs for training; Plus/Team/Enterprise tiers don't.
resources.note: Best at: broad general tasks, voice mode, integration with other tools.

resources.title: Claude
resources.description: General-purpose LLM. 200K token context window, strong at analysis, summarization, and writing. Pro tier does not use inputs for training.
resources.note: Best at: long document analysis, careful reasoning, writing that needs nuance.

resources.title: Gemini
resources.description: Google's LLM. Deep integration with Google Workspace – Docs, Sheets, Gmail, Drive. Free tier available.
resources.note: Best at: working with Google Workspace files you already have.

resources.title: Elicit
resources.description: AI research assistant. Searches 138 million indexed papers, extracts data across studies, supports systematic review workflows.
resources.note: The best dedicated tool for literature work right now. I use it for every review.

resources.title: Semantic Scholar
resources.description: Free academic search engine with AI-generated summaries and citation context. Nonprofit – no commercial motive shaping results.

resources.title: NotebookLM
resources.description: Upload documents, get AI analysis grounded in those sources only. 1M token context window, structured data extraction, audio summaries. Free.
resources.note: Good for synthesis across large document sets. Answers stay grounded in what you upload.

resources.title: Consensus
resources.description: Ask plain-language research questions, get answers synthesized from peer-reviewed papers with evidence shown behind each claim.

resources.title: Research Rabbit
resources.description: Visual citation mapping. Start with seed papers, find related work that keyword search misses. Free.

resources.title: scite.ai
resources.description: Shows how papers have been cited – whether subsequent work supported, contradicted, or just mentioned findings.

resources.title: Granola
resources.description: Ambient meeting transcription from laptop mic. No bot joins the call. Reusable templates, cross-meeting search.
resources.note: Records without other participants knowing. Understand consent implications before deploying.

resources.title: ChatGPT Record Mode
resources.description: Records ambient audio through device microphone via the ChatGPT app. No bot, no notification to other participants.
resources.note: Same consent implications as Granola – other people in the room don't know.

resources.title: Microsoft Copilot in Teams
resources.description: Built into Microsoft 365. Summarizes meetings, drafts follow-ups, searches chat history. Data stays within your organization's tenant.

resources.title: WHO EIOS 2.0
resources.description: AI-powered platform for early detection of public health threats. Processes news, social media, and radio (speech-to-text) across 110+ countries.
resources.note: Free for public health agencies. Directly relevant to FETP and national surveillance programs.

resources.title: HealthMap
resources.description: Automated disease surveillance using NLP to analyze real-time web data – news reports, health forums, official alerts.

resources.title: ATLAS.ti
resources.description: AI-assisted qualitative coding for thematic analysis. Participant data may be processed through AI models during analysis.
resources.note: NVivo and MAXQDA also embed generative AI now. Check data handling policies before uploading participant data.

resources.title: Turnitin
resources.description: AI writing detection integrated into plagiarism checking. False positive rates are a known issue – flags should prompt review, not automatic rejection.

resources.title: Claude Code
resources.description: Command-line AI coding assistant. Reads your codebase, writes and edits files, runs commands. Works in your terminal, not a separate app.

resources.title: Cursor
resources.description: AI-native code editor built on VS Code. Autocomplete, chat, and codebase-aware editing. Lower barrier to entry than command-line tools.

resources.title: Hugging Face
resources.description: Open-source ML model hub. Host, share, and deploy models. Where most open-weight AI models live.

callout.title: Not listed: Perplexity
callout.body: Perplexity is widely used but excluded here due to ongoing concerns about content scraping and publisher relations.
